neighborhoods.name: Deep Ellum
neighborhoods.description: Dallas's creative hub — murals, dive bars, and the best punk and indie scene in DFW.

neighborhoods.name: Lower Greenville
neighborhoods.description: A diverse strip of bars and restaurants with something for every taste.

neighborhoods.name: Oak Cliff
neighborhoods.description: Bishop Arts, the Kessler, and a growing scene of local neighborhood spots.

neighborhoods.name: Bishop Arts
neighborhoods.description: Oak Cliff's heart — boutique shops, wine bars, and intimate live music rooms.

neighborhoods.name: Lakewood
neighborhoods.description: East Dallas residential with a handful of legendary local bars and the Balcony Club.

neighborhoods.name: Fort Worth
neighborhoods.description: From the Stockyards to downtown — honky-tonk, blues, and Texas music roots.

neighborhoods.name: Downtown Dallas
neighborhoods.description: Arts District, Main Street, and the Bryan Pkwy corridor.

venues.name: The Goat Dallas
venues.description: Dallas dive blues bar. No pretension, just good music and cold drinks. Fave of the neighborhood for 20+ years.

venues.name: Lee Harvey's
venues.description: Legendary Dallas dive bar with a jukebox that tops any playlist. Cheap drinks, no attitude, always a cast of characters.

venues.name: Double Wide
venues.description: A fine Dallas bar with drinks that hit harder than your dad. Local and touring punk and rock acts.

venues.name: Single Wide
venues.description: Deep Ellum's favorite low-key bar. Cheap drinks, good people, local bands most nights.

venues.name: The Blue Light
venues.description: Deep Ellum institution. The Blue Light has been the home for rock, blues, and country in the neighborhood for decades.

venues.name: The Foundry
venues.description: Backyard patio bar with fried chicken and live music. A hidden gem off the beaten path.

venues.name: The Balcony Club
venues.address: 1825 Abrams Road, Suite B, Dallas, TX 75206
venues.description: Dallas's coziest live jazz spot. Intimate room, excellent musicians, BYOB allowed. A hidden gem in Lakewood.

venues.name: The Barley House
venues.description: East Dallas neighborhood spot with decent food, good beer selection, and live music on weekends.

venues.name: Bowlski's / Lakewood Theater
venues.description: An old theater space repurposed as a bowling alley and live music venue. Big room, big acts for cheap.

venues.name: Granada Theater
venues.description: Dallas's best mid-size concert venue. A converted 1940s theater with stellar acoustics and a lineup that punches way above its weight — national acts in an intimate room where every seat is close.

venues.name: Truck Yard Dallas
venues.description: A movable feast of food trucks, cold beer, and live music under the Texas sky. Summer nights don't get better.

venues.name: Curious Mechanic
venues.description: Bishop Arts coffee shop by day, bar by night with local acoustic acts and open mic Tuesdays.

venues.name: Wild Horse Saloon
venues.description: Fort Worth's premier country music and line dancing venue. A little bit Nashville in the Stockyards.

venues.name: Lenny C's
venues.description: BBQ meets live music in the heart of the Stockyards. No frills, good Texas music.

venues.name: Madness at the Woods
venues.address: 2453 N Main St, Fort Worth, TX 76164
venues.description: Fort Worth's underground music scene. Local and touring punk, metal, and rock acts in a small room.

venues.name: Kessler Theater
venues.description: Oak Cliff's premier intimate concert venue. A restored 1940s movie house hosting local and national acts in a room where you can see the performer's eyes. The beating heart of the Bishop Arts music scene.
venues.hours: Box office opens 1 hour before showtime

venues.name: Across the Way
venues.description: Oak Cliff's go-to for live music near the Bishop Arts strip. Outdoor patio and a solid jukebox too.
